holding_period_lt_under_5yr.description: Fraction of net LT gains held 1-5 years (vs 5+ years). Higher-income filers tend to hold longer.
sources: JCT estimates of holding period distributions
holding_period_lt_under_5yr.note: Values are calibration placeholders. Reform targets gains held <5 years for ordinary rate treatment.

empirical.source: IRS SOI Sales of Capital Assets, Table 4A (2015)
empirical.method: Ratio of classifiable LT gains held 1-5yr to total classifiable LT gains. SOCA does not provide AGI breakdowns for holding periods, so a single aggregate fraction is used.
limitations: 71% of LT gains have 'Period not determinable' — fraction computed from 29% of classifiable gains only
Data is from 2015 (most recent SOCA available); structural ratios assumed stable
No AGI-level stratification available from SOCA Table 4
empirical.note: Run 'python -m src.validation.calibration_validate' to populate empirical values

gross_loss_ratio.description: Fraction of gross LT gains offset by harvested losses. If 0.85, only 15% of gross gains survive as net — the rest are deliberately sold losers used to offset.
gross_loss_ratio.note: Most investors with meaningful portfolios engage in year-end tax-loss harvesting, selling losers to offset winners. Higher-income filers harvest more aggressively (larger portfolios, better advisors, stronger incentive at higher marginal rates). Under reform, these stranded losses can no longer offset reclassified gains ($3K/yr cap).

empirical.method: Implied_Loss_Ratio = (LT_Loss + LT_Loss_Carryover) / (LT_Gain + LT_Loss + LT_Loss_Carryover). This is a lower-bound estimate of harvesting intensity computed from net amounts reported on Schedule D.
limitations: Table 1.4A reports NET gains and NET losses, not gross transaction-level data
Loss carryover reflects prior-year activity, not current-year harvesting alone
Netting within ST/LT categories obscures true gross loss volume
empirical.note: Run 'python -m src.validation.calibration_validate' to populate empirical values
